must_detect.matched_text: Acquisition linkage (Project Seroja): pending definitive Perjanjian Jual Beli Saham (SPA) to acquire 60% of PT Karya Data Sentral on 22 July 2026 for IDR 152,000,000,000; conditions precedent include “no Material Adverse Effect” on the payments platform
Term sheet variance: earn-out adjusted by ±5% if average daily processed volume remains below baseline for >10 consecutive days post-closing

STRICTLY NON-PUBLIC — Board Incident Memo, PT PayNusa Teknologi Tbk — Deal Codename: Project Seroja
Embargo until: 15 July 2026, 09:00 WIB; do not distribute beyond Board and OJKN liaison
Incident summary (operational/cyber): 11 June 2026 02:14 WIB, core payment switch at Data Center Serpong failed due to malware-triggered reboot loop; approx 28% transaction throughput degraded, IDR 78,500,000,000 gross settlement delayed
No customer funds lost; 6,213 wallets auto-locked pending KYC revalidation; temporary downtime reported on public social media at 07:00 WIB
Contact: Ibu Sari Lestari, Chief Risk Officer, sari.lestari@paynusa.co.id; hotline +62 811-9000-123 (internal only)
Regulatory context: As a BEN-listed issuer, we must file keterbukaan informasi to OJKN/BEN under POJKN No.31/2015 within 2 business days; draft IR note attached for review
Acquisition linkage (Project Seroja): pending definitive Perjanjian Jual Beli Saham (SPA) to acquire 60% of PT Karya Data Sentral on 22 July 2026 for IDR 152,000,000,000; conditions precedent include “no Material Adverse Effect” on the payments platform
Management assessment: this event is not reasonably expected to result in a MAC/MAE under the SPA, as transactions recovered to 96% within 18 hours and no data exfiltration observed
Term sheet variance: earn-out adjusted by ±5% if average daily processed volume remains below baseline for >10 consecutive days post-closing
Public-source context: Harian Bursa Nusantara (29 May 2026) speculated on a generic “data center upgrade” at a fintech issuer, but did not identify our company
Employment/HR: 14 contractors from PT Mitra Sukses Data were placed on paid leave pending forensics; no layoffs are contemplated
ESG and privacy: initial read indicates no personal data transferred cross-border; UU PDP notification threshold not triggered, but we will prepare a draft notice in case later forensics differ
Checklist items (lowercase): update runbook, refresh edr signatures, confirm dr site capacity, prep ojkn q&a, coordinate ben halting protocol (if needed)
Draft investor-relations line (non-public): “operational disruption contained; no impact to July disbursement guidance; SPA timeline unchanged.”
Research note (internal): scenario analysis shows worst-case revenue impact of 1.9% for Q3; base case neutral with catch-up fees; sensitivity to MDR reduction 25 bps
please note the wellness spa day is postponed and unrelated to the share purchase agreement
Approvals required before embargo date: Pak Arman Widodo (Chair), Bu Dina Pramesti (Komisaris Independen), and OJKN liaison at Kantor PT PayNusa (non-public channel)
